title: Business sales executives
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate marketing policies and programs, such as determining the demand for products and services offered by a firm and its competitors, and identify potential customers. Develop pricing strategies with the goal of maximizing the firm's profits or share of the market while ensuring the firm's customers are satisfied. Oversee product development or monitor trends that indicate the need for new products and services. Plan, direct, or coordinate the actual distribution or movement of a product or service to the customer. Coordinate sales distribution by establishing sales territories, quotas, and goals and establish training programs for sales representatives. Analyze sales statistics gathered by staff to determine sales potential and inventory requirements and monitor the preferences of customers. (Based on 32 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with customers and engineers to assess equipment needs and determine system requirements.
Plan and modify product configurations to meet customer needs, and arrange for demonstrations or trial installations of equipment.
Maintain sales forecasting reports and document account activities, generating reports of business transactions with customers and suppliers.
Train team members in customer applications of technologies and provide ongoing technical support to customers.
Identify resale opportunities and develop sales plans to introduce products in new markets while securing and renewing orders.
Develop, present, or respond to proposals for specific customer requirements and prepare or review detailed design drawings for installations.
Visit prospective buyers to demonstrate products, explain pricing, and evaluate needs, while keeping informed on industry news and trends.
Create sales contracts, quotes, or presentations, and provide customers with information about delivery schedules, warranties, and funding options.
Research and identify potential customers for products or services, using business directories and participating in trade shows or conferences.
Monitor market conditions and competitors' products and prices to inform sales strategies and improve product offerings.
Collaborate with sales teams to promote company products and provide sales support while maintaining customer records using automated systems.
Negotiate prices or terms of sales or service agreements, and recommend products based on customers' needs and interests.
Attend trade shows and seminars to promote products or learn about industry developments, and provide customers with product samples and catalogs.
Prepare sales order tickets and submit them for processing of client-requested transactions, while completing sales or expense reports.
Calculate costs for billings or commissions and estimate savings from new products or services, informing customers about these benefits.
Consult with buying personnel regarding the types of products or services expected to be in demand.
Conduct economic or commercial surveys to identify potential markets for products or services.
Evaluate the financial aspects of product development, including budgets, expenditures, and profit-loss projections.
Identify, develop, or evaluate marketing strategy based on establishment objectives, market characteristics, and cost factors.
Develop pricing strategies that balance firm objectives and customer satisfaction.
Direct hiring, training, or performance evaluations of marketing or sales staff and oversee their daily activities.
Negotiate contracts with vendors or distributors to manage product distribution and develop distribution strategies.
Prepare budgets and approve budget expenditures.
Oversee regional and local sales managers and their staff.
Confer with potential customers regarding equipment needs and advise on types of equipment to purchase.
Direct and coordinate activities involving sales of products, services, or commodities.
Conduct market research analysis to identify trends, opportunities, and competitor performance.
Collect and analyze data on customer demographics, preferences, and needs to identify potential markets.
Monitor industry statistics and follow trends relevant to sales and marketing.
Negotiate and arrange transport of goods with shipping companies.
Prepare shipping documentation and verify adherence to customs and regulatory requirements.
Measure the effectiveness of marketing, advertising, and communications programs and strategies.
Prepare reports of findings to translate complex data into actionable insights.
Direct trained survey interviewers and evaluate data collection methods.
Advise clients on transportation and payment methods to facilitate efficient business operations.
Present drafts and ideas to clients and discuss changes or revisions to advertising copy with them.
Write articles, sales letters, and other promotional materials to market products and services effectively.
Conduct research to determine which product features should be promoted and analyze advertising trends.
Develop and execute advertising campaigns, collaborating with creative directors and art directors.
Prepare promotional plans and sales literature and consult with clients on advertising strategies.
Provide clients with estimates for advertising costs and maintain records of their account bases.
Identify new advertising markets and analyze customer preferences to propose relevant products.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to customers to promote products and increase existing advertising.
Gather and coordinate materials for bid processes and oversee the contracting for advertising work.
Maintain knowledge of industry trends and collect historical data to support current advertising efforts.
Analyze areas of potential risk to the assets, earning capacity, or success of organizations, and develop or implement risk-assessment models or methodologies.
Evaluate and compare the relative quality of various securities in a given industry and analyze new legislation to determine its impact on risk exposure.
Create, manage, or automate orders or invoices, using order management or invoicing software, and calculate revenue, sales, and expenses using financial accounting software.
Determine location for product listings to maximize exposure to online traffic and promote products in online communities through various marketing channels.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel and provide staff with assistance in performing difficult or complicated duties.
Confer with company officials to develop methods and procedures to increase sales, expand markets, and promote business, including planning work schedules.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met and prepare sales and inventory reports for management and budget departments.
Direct and supervise employees engaged in sales, inventory-taking, and specific services, while examining merchandise for correct pricing and display.
Investigate products or markets to determine areas of opportunity or viability for merchandising specific products using online or offline sources.
Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities with other departments to improve operational efficiency.
